target.title: perf(agents): reuse media tool availability during tool prep
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse the optional media tool factory plan for the image tool instead of resolving image availability separately.
- Let image/video/music generation tool factories skip their registration-time availability scan when createOpenClawTools already resolved availability.
- Use the same effective media tool config for the plan and constructed media tools.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Repeated warm `createOpenClawCodingTools` prep was spending about 29ms each registering `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and `music_generate` because those factories repeated availability checks already resolved by the OpenClaw media factory plan.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `media-tool-availability-pr4c`, Node 25.9.0 through repo `pnpm exec tsx`; warm in-process tool prep probe after one warmup call.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a real local `node`/`tsx` probe that imports `createOpenClawCodingTools`, records `recordToolPrepStage` timings, discards one warmup call, then records 30 warm samples; wrapped with `/usr/bin/time -l`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
total p50 103.68ms, p95 110.16ms
openclaw-tools:image_generate registration p50 0.005ms
openclaw-tools:video_generate registration p50 0.002ms
openclaw-tools:music_generate registration p50 0.002ms
/usr/bin/time -l max RSS 850575360 bytes
```

Observed result after fix: Single-call warm p50 improved by about 84ms versus the previous same-probe baseline, from 187.74ms p50 to 103.68ms p50 (~45%). The three generation registration stages dropped from about 29ms each to near-zero. Remaining warm prep cost is mostly plugin-tools (~73ms p50) and image/media availability planning (~30ms p50).

What was not tested: Full end-to-end provider media generation was not run; this PR only changes registration-time availability reuse, while direct factory calls and execution-time provider resolution remain covered by targeted tests.

## Verification
- pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core
- pnpm build
- pnpm check:changed --staged

target.title: feat(feishu): expose non-bot @mentions in inbound conversation metadata
target.body:
## Problem

In a Feishu group chat with multiple bots (e.g. `龙虾小子` + `Arbiter`), when a user types `@Arbiter hello`, the `@Arbiter` is a Feishu rich-text mention element — **not plain text**. The receiving bot only sees the stripped message body (`hello`), with no signal that a *different* bot was addressed.

Result: both bots respond, even though only one was addressed.

## Root Cause

The Feishu plugin already has full mention data in `event.message.mentions`, and `normalizeMentions()` converts mention keys to `<at user_id="...">` tags in the message body. However, this normalized form still doesn't give the agent a clean, structured list of *who* was mentioned. The agent needs to pattern-match against raw HTML tags in an untrusted string — fragile and easy to miss.

## Fix

Surface the structured mention list directly in the **trusted inbound conversation-info metadata block** that agents already read.

**Three small changes:**

1. `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` — collect all non-self mentions from `event.message.mentions` and pass them as `Mentions` in the template context.
2. `src/auto-reply/templating.ts` — add `Mentions?: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>` to `TemplateContext`.
3. `src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts` — include `mentions` in the conversation-info user-role block when present.

## Result

When a user sends `@Arbiter hello` in a group, the receiving bot now sees:

```json
{
  "message_id": "...",
  "is_group_chat": true,
  "mentions": [{"id": "ou_arbiter_openid", "name": "Arbiter"}]
}
```

The agent can compare `mentions[*].name` (or `id`) against its known sibling bots and emit `NO_REPLY` immediately — no regex on message body required.

## Notes

- Only non-self mentions are included (the bot's own mention is already captured by `was_mentioned`).
- The field is omitted entirely when there are no non-self mentions (zero overhead for typical messages).
- No breaking changes; existing agents that don't use `mentions` are unaffected.

---

*Discovered while running two bots (龙虾小子 + Arbiter) in the same Feishu group. Both would respond to `@Arbiter` messages because the mention wasn't detectable from the agent's perspective.*

target.title: fix(telegram): apply reply_to only to first stream preview to avoid 'Deleted message'
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** During Telegram streaming, the reply header briefly shows "Deleted message" when tool boundaries cause `forceNewMessage()` to create new preview messages.
- **Root cause:** After `forceNewMessage()`, the new preview message was also sent with `reply_to_message_id` pointing to the user's original message. When the first preview was archived and deleted, Telegram briefly showed "Deleted message" for subsequent previews that also tried to reply to the same user message.
- **Fix:** Track whether `reply_to_message_id` has been applied with `hasAppliedReply` flag. Only the first preview message gets the reply context; subsequent messages after `forceNewMessage()` are sent without a reply target.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Telegram)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39718
- Related to #32924 (which fixes reasoning lane deletions, different root cause)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- The first assistant streaming reply in Telegram will still quote/reply to the user's message
- Subsequent assistant messages in the same stream (e.g., after tool use) will NOT quote/reply to the user's message
- This prevents the brief "Deleted message" artifact that appeared during streaming

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Steps

1. Enable streaming in Telegram
2. Ask the agent a question that triggers tool use (multiple response segments)
3. **Before fix:** Brief "Deleted message" appears in reply header during streaming
4. **After fix:** First message replies to user, subsequent messages have no reply context

### Evidence

- [x] New test: `applies reply_to_message_id only to the first message, not after forceNewMessage (#39718)`
- [x] All 28 tests in `draft-stream.test.ts` pass

## Human Verification

- Verified: First message gets reply_to, subsequent messages do not
- Edge cases: Materialize still uses reply context correctly for the permanent message
- What I did not verify: Live Telegram testing (no Telegram access)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes (slightly changed UX for multi-segment responses)
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to revert: Revert PR commit
- Known bad symptoms: Users might miss the reply context on subsequent messages (acceptable trade-off)

target.title: fix(codex): complete dynamic tool diagnostics
target.body:
Fixes #83474.

## Summary

This fixes a Codex app-server dynamic tool bookkeeping gap where `item/tool/call` requests can return a dynamic tool response, but OpenClaw does not emit a terminal tool diagnostic or clear the tracked dynamic tool call id at that request boundary.

The observed production symptom was a successful no-output `bash` dynamic tool result shown in the UI while the gateway kept reporting:

```text
reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=... recovery=none
```

The change makes the dynamic tool request path:

- emit `tool.execution.started` when the dynamic tool request begins;
- emit terminal `tool.execution.completed` / `tool.execution.error` when the dynamic tool response returns or throws;
- delete the tracked dynamic tool call id in `finally`;
- cover the diagnostic behavior in `run-attempt.test.ts`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83474
- Related #42112, #8288
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server dynamic `bash` tool completion was visible to the user, but gateway diagnostics kept the session in `blocked_tool_call` with `activeTool=bash` and a redacted active tool call id.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu/Linux desktop, installed OpenClaw / `@openclaw/codex` 2026.5.12 for the pre-fix failure; local patched checkout `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (78d4183)` for after-fix diagnostic activity and patched-gateway transport attempts on May 18, 2026.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --check --experimental-strip-types extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
pnpm dlx node@24 ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
node --import tsx <redacted-diagnostic-activity-proof-script>
OPENCLAW_PROOF_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node --import tsx scripts/repro/codex-dynamic-tool-diagnostics-live-proof.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

The patched boundary now emits the terminal event that clears gateway activity state. I validated the redacted activity transition directly against the same `diagnostic-run-activity` tracker used by gateway diagnostics:

```json
{
  "afterStart": {
    "activeWorkKind": "tool_call",
    "activeToolName": "bash",
    "activeToolCallId": "tool-call-redacted",
    "activeToolAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressReason": "tool:bash:started"
  },
  "afterCompleted": {
    "lastProgressAgeMs": 0,
    "lastProgressReason": "tool:bash:ended"
  }
}
```

After the terminal diagnostic, the gateway activity snapshot no longer contains `activeWorkKind=tool_call`, `activeToolName=bash`, or `activeToolCallId`.

Patched local gateway transport attempt, redacted:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (78d4183)
[proof] setup
[proof] pair-device
[proof] start-gateway
[gateway] agent model: codex/gpt-5.4 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: codex)
[gateway] ready
[proof] gateway-call-agent
Gateway call failed: GatewayTransportError: gateway timeout after 120000ms
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:<redacted-port>
Config: <redacted-temp-config-path>
```

- Observed result after fix: Source-level patched request-boundary coverage and diagnostic activity proof show that terminal `tool.execution.completed`/`tool.execution.error` clears the active `bash` tool state that produced the stalled diagnostics. The local patched gateway starts from commit `78d4183`, but the live Codex transport did not reach tool execution in the patched runtime attempt.
- What was not tested: A successful patched live Codex `bash` tool request completing end-to-end through `gateway call agent`. The patched live attempts either disconnected before tool execution or timed out before a terminal tool event, so this PR still relies on the focused request-boundary test plus the gateway activity transition proof unless a maintainer can reproduce the live transport path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
2026-05-18T07:52:04.249+02:00 [diagnostic] stalled session: sessionId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> state=processing age=143s queueDepth=0 reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeWorkKind=tool_call lastProgress=codex_app_server:notification:thread/tokenUsage/updated lastProgressAge=141s activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=<redacted> activeToolAge=148s recovery=none
2026-05-18T07:57:04.291+02:00 [diagnostic] stalled session: sessionId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted> state=processing age=443s queueDepth=0 reason=blocked_tool_call classification=blocked_tool_call activeWorkKind=tool_call lastProgress=codex_app_server:notification:thread/tokenUsage/updated lastProgressAge=441s activeTool=bash activeToolCallId=<redacted> activeToolAge=448s recovery=none
2026-05-18T07:57:27.172+02:00 [gateway] draining 2 active task(s) and 1 active embedded run(s) before restart with timeout 300000ms
2026-05-18T07:57:57.185+02:00 [gateway] still draining 2 active task(s) and 1 active embedded run(s) before restart
openclaw-gateway.service: State 'stop-sigterm' timed out. Killing.
```

No secrets, auth tokens, profile hashes, local usernames, session ids, full local paths, ports, or temp config paths are included in the proof above.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `item/tool/call` branch tracked dynamic tool request state, but did not emit a terminal diagnostic event or clear the tracked dynamic tool call id at that request boundary.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered dynamic tool lookup and error behavior, but not the gateway-facing diagnostic start/end contract for this response-boundary path.
- Contributing context (if known): Related diagnostic stalls existed in the same broad area, but this report appears to be a distinct response-boundary variant.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Dynamic `item/tool/call` handling emits start and terminal diagnostic events and clears tracked dynamic tool ids.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is at a narrow app-server request boundary; the focused test exercises that boundary without depending on live Codex transport reliability.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Added in this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-backed OpenClaw sessions should stop reporting a completed dynamic tool as still active after the app-server returns the dynamic tool response.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[item/tool/call] -> dynamic tool response -> no terminal diagnostic -> activeTool remains set

After:
[item/tool/call] -> tool.execution.started -> dynamic tool response -> terminal diagnostic -> activeTool cleared
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu/Linux desktop
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.0 locally; focused Vitest run under Node 24.15.0 because host Node 22 cannot load the repo Vitest config
- Model/provider: `openai-codex` / `codex/gpt-5.4` and `codex/gpt-5.5` attempts
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw Gateway with bundled Codex app-server plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): local token auth, loopback gateway, Codex app-server `mode: yolo`, temp state/config paths redacted

### Steps

1. Reproduce the stalled active-tool diagnostics on installed OpenClaw 2026.5.12.
2. Apply the patch and run focused static/type/test validation.
3. Validate the gateway diagnostic activity transition with terminal `tool.execution.completed`.
4. Attempt a patched local gateway live Codex `bash` run; record timeout/disconnect outcome without exposing secrets.

### Expected

- Terminal dynamic tool diagnostics clear `activeTool=bash` and its tool call id.

### Actual

- Diagnostic activity proof clears the active `bash` tool state after the terminal event.
- Patched live Codex transport did not reach tool execution in this environment.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Pre-fix stalled active `bash` diagnostics; patched diagnostic activity transition; focused run-attempt regression test.
- Edge cases checked: Terminal completed/error event handling and tracked dynamic id cleanup in the patched request-boundary code path.
- What I did **not** verify: Successful patched live Codex `bash` tool completion through `gateway call agent`, because live transport timed out or disconnected before tool execution.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Prematurely clearing active tool diagnostics could hide a still-active tool call.
  - Mitigation: The patch emits terminal cleanup only after the dynamic tool response returns or throws, and the focused test covers the diagnostic event sequence.
- Risk: Live after-fix transport proof is incomplete.
  - Mitigation: The proof calls out the gap explicitly and keeps the before logs, focused regression, and activity-transition evidence redacted and reproducible.

## Validation

Passed:

```bash
node --check --experimental-strip-types extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
pnpm dlx node@24 ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
```

Focused Vitest result under Node 24:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  164 passed (164)
Duration  43.22s
```

Note: the host's system Node 22.22.0 cannot load the repo Vitest config because `path.matchesGlob` throws `Missing internal module 'internal/deps/brace-expansion'`. Running the same focused test with temporary Node 24.15.0 avoids that local runtime issue.

## AI Assistance

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the issue evidence, searched for related upstream reports before filing, made the code changes locally, redacted the proof, and ran the validation commands listed above.

target.title: fix(config): make models.providers.<id>.models optional to allow credential-only entries
target.body:
## Problem

When a user configures `models.providers.openai` with a custom `baseUrl` (e.g. an OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint) but without a `models` array, OpenClaw's config validation rejects the entry with **`INVALID_CONFIG`**:

```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "openai": {
        "baseUrl": "https://api.gptsapi.net/v1",
        "apiKey": "${OPENAI_API_KEY}",
        "api": "openai-completions"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

On a live gateway a failed reload is silently ignored (the gateway keeps running with the stale config). The stale config has no `models.providers.openai` entry, so `providerConfig?.baseUrl` in `resolveRemoteEmbeddingBearerClient` is `undefined`, and the embedding system falls back to the hard-coded `DEFAULT_OPENAI_BASE_URL = "https://api.openai.com/v1"` instead of the intended custom endpoint. The user then sees `TypeError: fetch failed` when the request reaches the wrong host.

## Root Cause

`ModelProviderSchema` in `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` declares `models` as a **required** field:

```ts
models: z.array(ModelDefinitionSchema),  // required — fails if omitted
```

Users who configure a provider entry solely for credentials/baseUrl (no custom models) have no reason to include an empty `models: []`. Making the field required creates an unnecessary footgun.

## Fix

Change the field declaration to use `.default([])`:

```ts
models: z.array(ModelDefinitionSchema).default([]),
```

The parsed output still always contains an array (never `undefined`), so all downstream code that iterates over models remains safe. Configs that omit `models` now pass validation and the gateway picks up the custom `baseUrl` correctly.

## Tests

**`src/config/zod-schema.model-provider.test.ts`** (new file, 3 tests):
- ✅ Provider entry without `models` field passes validation and defaults to `[]`
- ✅ Provider entry with explicit `models: []` passes validation  
- ✅ `baseUrl` is preserved when `models` is omitted

**`src/memory/embeddings.test.ts`** (1 test added):
- ✅ `createEmbeddingProvider` routes to `models.providers.openai.baseUrl` when no `remote.baseUrl` override is present

Fixes #39589

target.title: feat(feishu): add a read-only mode for group chats
target.body:
## What this changes

This PR adds a Feishu config switch for teams that want OpenClaw to **observe group chats without ever replying into them**.

New config:

- `channels.feishu.disableGroupReplies: true`

When enabled:

- Feishu **group messages are still received** and routed into the agent
- Feishu **DM replies continue to work normally**
- Feishu **group replies are blocked at the program level**
- Explicit outbound Feishu group targets such as `chat:oc_xxx` / `group:oc_xxx` are also blocked

## Why

Some deployments want the bot to:

- read group history or live group traffic
- keep private chat interactions enabled
- avoid accidental replies in groups, even if the model is prompted, mentioned, or socially engineered

This moves that behavior from a prompt-level convention to a **channel-level guardrail**.

## Implementation

- add `disableGroupReplies` to the Feishu config schema and channel config schema
- use a no-op reply dispatcher for Feishu group dispatch when the flag is enabled
- reject explicit outbound Feishu group targets when group replies are disabled
- document the config in `docs/channels/feishu.md`

## Example

```json5
{
  channels: {
    feishu: {
      enabled: true,
      groupPolicy: "allowlist",
      groupAllowFrom: ["oc_group_a", "oc_group_b"],
      requireMention: true,
      disableGroupReplies: true
    }
  }
}
```

Passed locally.

## Notes

This intentionally keeps inbound group visibility intact while making outbound group replies impossible unless the config is changed.

target.title: feat: add repotype for skill/package structure enforcement
target.body:
## Summary

Adds [repotype](https://supernalintelligence.github.io/repotype/) for repository structure enforcement — a repo-level linter that complements existing code linters.

## What it does

- **Skills:** Ensures every skill has `SKILL.md`
- **Packages:** Validates `package.json` and `README.md` exist
- **Extensions:** Validates `manifest.json` exists
- **Permissive mode:** Starts with `unmatchedFiles: allow` (can tighten later)

## Why

With 50+ skills and many contributors, structural consistency matters. This catches missing files **before** they land in main.

## Usage

```bash
npx repotype validate .    # Check structure
npx repotype fix .         # Auto-fix issues
```

## CI integration (optional, not included in this PR)

```yaml
- run: npx repotype validate . --json
```

## Links

- [Repotype docs](https://supernalintelligence.github.io/repotype/)
- [OpenClaw example config](https://supernalintelligence.github.io/repotype/examples/openclaw-project/)

---

Built to Help Fight Entropy with ♥ from [Supernal Intelligence](https://supernal.ai)

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: 38d3d2a5ff7f820010ccd60578f35ecd6d800697.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts\n\n RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517\n\n Test Files  1 passed (1)\n      Tests  78 passed (78)\n   Duration  12.41s\n\n[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.72s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:

```text
$ node --input-type=module <Playwright proof runner>
{
  "screenshotPath": "/home/daein/.openclaw/workspace/logs/pr-watch/75727-ui-proof-2ce0267.png",

The message shape matched the normalized gateway result after the raw MEDIA transcript turn is replaced: one assistant text block plus one input_image content block, with no MEDIA directive left in visible text.

target.title: fix(telegram): implement poll_answer event routing into sessions
target.body:
## Problem

`poll_answer` is already declared in `allowed-updates.ts`, so Telegram delivers vote events to the bot, but no handler exists in the source. Vote events are silently dropped instead of reaching the agent session.

## Why it matters

Public polls sent via the `message` tool, or `openclaw message poll --poll-public`, produce no agent wakeup when users vote. There is no way to build vote-responsive flows without this.

## Root cause

`poll_answer` was added to `allowed-updates.ts` when the poll send action was implemented, but the corresponding inbound routing handler was never added. Half-implemented feature.

## What changed

- `extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts`, new module storing `pollId -> { chatId, question, options }` using `writeJsonAtomic`, versioned schema, capped at 100 entries, per-account registry files
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts`, `bot.on('poll_answer', ...)` handler that looks up the registry, builds human-readable vote text, and routes into the session via `enqueueSystemEvent`
- `extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.ts`, records poll registry entry on poll send, non-fatal if registry write fails
- `extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts`, adds `poll_answer` to allowed updates
- Tests for registry, action-runtime write, and bot event routing

## What did NOT change

- Anonymous poll behavior is unchanged, Telegram does not send `poll_answer` for anonymous polls
- Poll send API and schema unchanged
- No other event types affected

## Behavior note

Only activates for non-anonymous, public polls, `pollPublic: true` or `--poll-public`. This is a Telegram API constraint.

## Security impact

Adds a new inbound event handler for updates Telegram was already delivering per `allowed-updates.ts`. No new surface exposed. Voter identity comes from Telegram's authenticated update payload.

## Test plan

- Unit tests: `poll-registry.test.ts`, store, retrieve, prune at 100 entries
- Integration: `bot.test.ts`, routes vote into session, ignores missing registry entry gracefully
- Integration: `action-runtime.test.ts`, registry written on poll send
- Human verification: send a public poll via `openclaw message poll --poll-public`, vote, confirm agent session receives wakeup

## Prior art

- PR #18122, merged, fixed poll action wiring
- PR #17357, closed stale, broader poll feature, this is a targeted follow-up completing what was left half-done

## AI disclosure

Implementation drafted with AI assistance, OpenClaw agent 🦞.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Public Telegram poll votes (`poll_answer`) now route into the originating Telegram session instead of being silently dropped.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu VPS, PR head commit `4a46756dc2`, patched Telegram extension source, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the patched PR head:

```console
✓ extension-telegram extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts (74 tests)
  ✓ routes poll_answer events into the originating session
✓ extension-telegram extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts (2 tests)
✓ extension-telegram extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts (57 tests)

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       133 passed (133)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 89.66s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The patched handler resolved the stored poll registry entry and enqueued the expected originating-session event: `Telegram poll vote: "Ready?" — Ada Lovelace (@ada) voted: No`. The send path also persisted the poll registry entry, and missing registry entries were ignored gracefully.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram Desktop visual proof was not run in this local pass; this PR still has the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label for maintainer-run visible proof if required.

target.title: fix: prevent MEDIA: false-positive extraction from indented code blocks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #69311

`splitMediaFromOutput()` incorrectly extracts `MEDIA:` directives from 4-space/tab indented code blocks because `parseFenceSpans()` only recognizes fenced (`\`\`\``/`~~~`) code blocks. This causes code lines to be silently deleted and spurious media attachment requests to be generated.

## Changes

### Fix A: Indented code block heuristic (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Added `isIndentedCodeLine()` function that detects lines starting with 4 spaces or tab, preceded by a blank line or another indented code line (CommonMark heuristic)
- Tracks `prevLineBlankOrIndentedCode` state through the line loop
- Skips MEDIA extraction for lines identified as indented code

### Fix A+: Whitespace folding protection (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Changed `cleanedText` construction to apply whitespace folding only to non-code lines
- Tracks `keptLineIsCode[]` parallel to `keptLines[]`
- Changed `.trim()` to `.replace(/^\\n+/, "").trimEnd()` to preserve leading indentation on first content line

### Fix C: Fence closing line validation (`src/markdown/fences.ts`)
- Added `/^\\s*$/.test(match[3])` check when validating closing fence lines
- Rejects closing fences with trailing non-whitespace text per CommonMark spec
- Affects 5 downstream consumers (all become more correct — strictly more conservative fence spans)

### Fix D: MEDIA_TOKEN_RE flag cleanup (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Removed dead `i` flag from `MEDIA_TOKEN_RE` regex
- The upstream gate `startsWith("MEDIA:")` is already case-sensitive, so lowercase never reaches the regex

## Test Plan

- **18 new tests** added across `src/media/parse.test.ts` (10) and `src/markdown/fences.test.ts` (8, new file)
- All 44 media/parse tests pass
- All 8 fences tests pass
- Regression: `npx vitest run src/media/ src/markdown/` — all pass

### Key test scenarios
- 4-space indented code block with MEDIA: (not extracted) ✅
- Tab indented code block with MEDIA: (not extracted) ✅
- Multi-line indented code block (not extracted) ✅
- Paragraph continuation after indented text (correctly extracted) ✅
- Fence exit then indented code (not extracted) ✅
- Real MEDIA + indented code (code indentation preserved) ✅
- Trailing text on closing fence (fence stays open) ✅
- Lowercase `media:` not extracted ✅

## Files Changed

| File | Lines | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `src/media/parse.ts` | +49/-6 | Fix A, A+, D |
| `src/markdown/fences.ts` | +5/-1 | Fix C |
| `src/media/parse.test.ts` | +172 | 10 new tests |
| `src/markdown/fences.test.ts` | +61 | 8 new tests (new file) |

**Total: 4 files, +288/-6**

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched. `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: refactor(config): extract GoogleChat schema into zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts
target.body:
## Summary

- Extract GoogleChat channel schemas (`GoogleChatDmSchema`, `GoogleChatGroupSchema`, `GoogleChatAccountSchema`, `GoogleChatConfigSchema`) from the monolithic `zod-schema.providers-core.ts` into a dedicated `zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts` module
- Follows the same pattern as `zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts` (already split out)
- Exports `BotLoopProtectionSchema` from `providers-core` since GoogleChat shares it with other channel schemas
- Updates the re-export barrel in `bundled-channel-config-schema.ts`

This is a continuation of the channel schema split pattern established by [#82007](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82007).

## Verification

### Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: code refactor only, no behavior change
Real environment tested: Linux, Node v22
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Schema export verification
node -e "
const tsx = require('tsx/cjs');
const { GoogleChatConfigSchema, GoogleChatDmSchema, GoogleChatGroupSchema, GoogleChatAccountSchema } = require('./src/config/zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts');
console.log('GoogleChatConfigSchema:', typeof GoogleChatConfigSchema);
console.log('GoogleChatDmSchema:', typeof GoogleChatDmSchema);
console.log('GoogleChatGroupSchema:', typeof GoogleChatGroupSchema);
console.log('GoogleChatAccountSchema:', typeof GoogleChatAccountSchema);
console.log('DmSchema parse:', GoogleChatDmSchema.safeParse({ enabled: true, policy: 'pairing' }).success);
const core = require('./src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts');
console.log('GoogleChat in providers-core:', Object.keys(core).filter(k => k.includes('GoogleChat')).length);
console.log('Other schemas in core:', Object.keys(core).filter(k => k.includes('ConfigSchema')).join(', '));
"

# Contract tests
timeout 120 pnpm test src/plugins/contracts/config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts src/config/config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts
```
Evidence after fix:
```
# Schema export verification
GoogleChatConfigSchema: object
GoogleChatDmSchema: object
GoogleChatGroupSchema: object
GoogleChatAccountSchema: object
DmSchema parse: true
GoogleChat in providers-core: 0 (should be 0)
Other schemas in core: DiscordConfigSchema, IMessageConfigSchema, IrcConfigSchema, MSTeamsConfigSchema, SignalConfigSchema, SlackConfigSchema, TelegramConfigSchema

# Contract tests
 ✓ |contracts-plugin| config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts (7 tests) — all passed
 ✓ |runtime-config| config.allowlist-requires-allowfrom.test.ts (15 tests) — all passed
 ✓ |contracts-plugin| plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts (4 tests) — all passed
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
```
Observed result after fix: GoogleChat schemas successfully extracted into their own module with 0 GoogleChat exports remaining in providers-core. All other 7 channel ConfigSchemas remain in providers-core. All 26 contract tests pass.
What was not tested: Full end-to-end gateway startup with GoogleChat channel enabled (requires Google OAuth credentials and webhook setup). CI will validate remaining lanes.

### Additional CI fixes applied
- Fixed `config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts`: regex now matches `providers-googlechat` export block; `toHaveLength(2)` → `3`
- Fixed import boundary in `zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts`: uses `ChannelBotLoopProtectionSchema` from `zod-schema.channels-config.ts` (neutral module) instead of importing from `zod-schema.providers-core.ts`
- Removed unused `SecretRefSchema` import from `zod-schema.providers-core.ts` (was only referenced by the extracted GoogleChat schemas)

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex no longer throws away the only saved app-server thread when the dynamic tool catalog changes. It keeps reusable Codex thread bindings per dynamic-tool fingerprint, so owner/non-owner tool availability flips start each catalog once and then resume the matching native thread.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. Default Codex app-server config could therefore register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint included the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools are filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change can alter the registered dynamic tool names. Before this PR, a mismatch cleared the single stored Codex binding, so alternating owner/non-owner turns could continuously create fresh native Codex sessions.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work only when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` can persist alternate Codex app-server thread bindings for the same OpenClaw session.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` selects a binding compatible with the current dynamic-tool fingerprint. If none exists, it starts a new binding without deleting other compatible bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` reselects the startup binding after dynamic tools are built, so context/startup decisions use the matching per-catalog Codex thread.
- Owner-only tools stay filtered for non-owner Codex thread starts.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching between owner and non-owner senders could change dynamic tool registration and repeatedly replace the saved Codex native thread binding.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Owner-only schemas are not sent to non-owner Codex thread starts.
- Owner and non-owner dynamic-tool catalogs get separate reusable Codex thread bindings: first flip starts the second catalog, later flips resume the matching existing thread.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not continuously replace Codex native sessions.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout on May 18, 2026. Private home/workspace paths and auth details are redacted from the older live-run transcript below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On current PR head `9413445bead7aff1dd939f55329045bcaefd038e`, ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`, `pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts`, and `env OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`. Earlier on this PR branch, ran real `pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 ...` turns through the Codex app-server harness.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output is embedded below under `Current-head verification`, `Production-code runtime probe`, and `Live Codex app-server smoke`.
- Observed result after fix: the owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume matching bindings on later flips; the non-owner `thread/start` payload omits owner-only tools; current-head tests and prompt snapshot check pass.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip is covered through the production Codex thread lifecycle code path and app-server tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source-level root cause is described above; no separate live before-run was captured because the original failure mode can create many relay child processes on a small machine.

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are runtime process/session churn in the Codex app-server path. Terminal/runtime output is the relevant proof artifact.

### Current-head verification

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
9413445bead7aff1dd939f55329045bcaefd038e

$ git diff --stat upstream/main...HEAD
11 files changed, 427 insertions(+), 99 deletions(-)

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  198 passed (198)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  112 passed (112)

$ pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)

$ env OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
```

The direct hook relay test needs a loopback listener; inside the Codex sandbox it fails with `listen EPERM: operation not permitted 127.0.0.1`, so that file was rerun outside the sandbox. The outside-sandbox run passed.

### Production-code runtime probe

This earlier probe directly exercised production `startOrResumeThread` and session-binding behavior on this PR, using owner tools `[message, cron]` and non-owner tools `[message]`:

```json
{
  "requestMethods": [
    "thread/start",
    "thread/start",
    "thread/resume",
    "thread/resume"
  ],
  "startDynamicTools": [
    [
      "message",
      "cron"
    ],
    [
      "message"
    ]
  ],
  "resumeThreadIds": [
    "thread-1",
    "thread-2"
  ],
  "storedBindings": [
    {
      "threadId": "thread-2",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    },
    {
      "threadId": "thread-1",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result: the owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume `thread-1` and `thread-2` on later flips. The non-owner `thread/start` payload only contained `message`, not `cron`.

### Live Codex app-server smoke

Earlier on this PR branch, I ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns with the same session id. That verified the actual app-server harness/auth path and same-thread resume behavior:

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"
```

## Regression Coverage

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts`: alternate dynamic-tool thread bindings persist beside the same OpenClaw session file.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: non-owner Codex thread starts omit owner-only dynamic tools, and owner/non-owner flips start each tool catalog once before resuming matching thread bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment. The focused affected suites above are green locally, and the latest push triggers exact-head GitHub CI.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head tested: `9d8852a46ca90538e1b1e2974c019530631edc88`.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
